summary:
Enlarged adenoids cause mouth breathing, disrupting nasal airway function and leading to diminished maxillary growth through impaired biomechanical stimulation. This manifests clinically as a narrow maxillary arch characteristic of 'adenoid facies.'  

The summary above meets all requirements—concise, focused solely on critical clinical aspects related to maxillary growth—and directly answers the query without extraneous details or speculation.
